examples.title: Token Passing Algorithm (Ring Topology)
examples.description: Simulates token passing mechanism used in Token Ring networks
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TokenPassing
BEGIN
  Initialize token = TRUE at Station 1
  WHILE network_active DO
    FOR each station in ring_order DO
      IF station.has_data_to_send AND token == TRUE THEN
        station.create_frame(data)
        station.attach_destination_address
        station.set_token_bit = FALSE
        Send frame to next_station
        token = FALSE
      ELSE
        Pass frame to next_station
      ENDIF
      
      IF frame.reaches_destination THEN
        station.extract_data
        station.send_acknowledgment
        station.set_token_bit = TRUE
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
  ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: Token Ring uses a special frame called a token that circulates on the ring. Only the station possessing the token can transmit data, preventing collisions. When data reaches destination, token is released back.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of stations in worst case
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) constant extra space for token and frame buffers

examples.title: CSMA/CD Algorithm (Bus Topology)
examples.description: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection used in Ethernet bus networks
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CSMA_CD
BEGIN
  WHILE station_has_data DO
    station.sense_carrier
    IF carrier_idle THEN
      station.wait_random_backoff
      station.transmit_frame
      WHILE transmitting DO
        IF collision_detected THEN
          station.stop_transmission
          station.send_jam_signal
          station.increment_collision_count
          IF collision_count > max_retries THEN
            station.discard_frame
            exit
          ENDIF
          wait exponential_backoff(collision_count)
          goto line 2  // retry
        ENDIF
      ENDWHILE
      station.wait_inter_frame_gap
    ELSE
      wait until carrier_idle
    ENDIF
  ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: CSMA/CD allows multiple stations to share a common bus medium. Stations listen before transmitting (Carrier Sense). If two stations transmit simultaneously, collision is detected and both wait random times before retrying.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for successful transmission, exponential backoff adds delay on collision
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for frame buffer and counters

examples.title: Network Classification by Scale
examples.description: Simple program to classify network type based on geographic coverage input
examples.code:
FUNCTION ClassifyNetwork(coverage_km, devices_count)
BEGIN
  IF coverage_km < 0.01 THEN
    RETURN "PAN - Personal Area Network"
  ELSE IF coverage_km < 2 THEN
    IF devices_count < 100 THEN
      RETURN "LAN - Local Area Network"
    ELSE
      RETURN "CAN - Campus Area Network"
    ENDIF
  ELSE IF coverage_km < 50 THEN
    RETURN "MAN - Metropolitan Area Network"
  ELSE
    RETURN "WAN - Wide Area Network"
  ENDIF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage:
PRINT ClassifyNetwork(0.005, 5)    // Output: PAN
PRINT ClassifyNetwork(0.5, 50)     // Output: LAN  
PRINT ClassifyNetwork(5, 500)      // Output: CAN
PRINT ClassifyNetwork(30, 2000)    // Output: MAN
PRINT ClassifyNetwork(500, 10000)  // Output: WAN
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how networks are classified by geographic coverage. Modern classification also considers device count and connectivity requirements.
